author: robzilla1738
message: Release v1.12.1 (build 127)

author: Robert Courson
message:
Post-v1.12.0 remediation: reimport data-loss, dict leaks, test+doc gaps (#177)

Fresh-eyes review of the v1.12.0 merge surfaced a small set of follow-ups:

- Re-import Terminal Config wiped user output triggers + per-host
  profiles: reimportTerminalConfig rebuilds settings via makeDefaults
  (defaults + imported VISUALS only). Capture and restore
  settings.profiles/triggers across the rebuild — they describe Harness
  behavior, not anything a source terminal config carries. (Targeted:
  reimport's existing reset semantics for other fields unchanged.)
- surfaceRemoteHosts and lastCommandDurations leaked one entry per pane
  ever created (only shrank on explicit OSC 7 nil-host / command-finish
  callbacks, which a killed pane never emits). Prune both to the live
  surface set in syncFromDaemon's structureChanged block, alongside
  terminalHosts.prune(keeping:).
- Settings round-trip coverage gap: the mega round-trip test omitted
  profiles + triggers, and there was no triggers equivalent of
  testSettingsRoundTripCarriesProfiles. Added both (a future custom
  encode(to:) that forgot them would now fail a test).
- KEYBINDINGS.md global-shortcuts table didn't list the v1.12.0 View-menu
  items; added Previous/Next Prompt (⌘↑/⌘↓) and Select Last Command
  Output (⌘⇧A).
- Locked the intentional replay/host behavior: OSC 7 fires
  onRemoteHostChange + onWorkingDirectoryChange during replay BY DESIGN
  (host/cwd are pane state, restored like title/grid — not world-facing
  effects like bells/queries that isReplaying suppresses), because the
  daemon PTY is the same process across an app restart so the replayed
  host == the current host and drives the correct reopened-pane profile.
  Added a comment so nobody gates it later, plus a regression test.

No coordinator-level reimport test: SessionCoordinator is a private-init
singleton with daemon deps; the 4-line capture/restore is guarded by the
settings round-trip tests proving the underlying persistence.

Suite 1947 green (+2).

author: robzilla1738
message: Release v1.12.0 (build 126)

author: Robert Courson
message:
Output triggers: highlight or notify on matching terminal output (#175)

Watch output for patterns and react — the iTerm2-class power feature
Harness lacked. Configured as a 'triggers' array in settings.json
(literal or regex; reload-on-save applies live):

  {"pattern": "ERROR", "action": "highlight"}
  {"pattern": "Tests? failed", "match": "regex", "action": "notify"}

Semantics:
- Lines scan as they COMPLETE (the cursor moved past them) — never
  mid-write, never on the alternate screen (TUIs redraw in place),
  and never from replayed history (a reopen must not re-fire
  notifications — #168's lesson).
- highlight spans ride the find-highlight machinery end to end
  (TerminalBufferMatch shape, viewport rebase, cell-overlay pass);
  both filter to the viewport so the plain-frame fast path still
  holds when nothing is on screen.
- notify routes through the host delegate to the same notification
  path as a program's OSC 9, with a 10s per-rule cooldown.

Bounded by design — the scan sits behind the parse path:
- 32-rule cap; literal rules match via NSString.range(of:) (no regex
  engine); invalid regexes are dropped with a warning.
- Per-batch budget (newest 256 lines) AND a rate budget (~2% of each
  100ms window of emulator-queue time): a sustained flood degrades to
  sampling recent lines instead of collapsing the drain rate. The
  gated A/B bench measured 122% worst-case overhead unbudgeted →
  2-11% (noise-level) with the budget; zero rules cost one nil check.
- Scan state is confined to the emulator's domain; results hop to
  main only when something matched.

NOTE for the #169 merge: receive() gains a replay flag there — thread
it into scanTriggers (the call sites pass false on this branch).

GUI rule editor deferred (settings.json + reload-on-save is the
power path; documented in MULTIPLEXER_GUIDE §13).

Tests: scanner compile/cap/escaping, scan-text column alignment with
wide chars, completed-line semantics, cooldown, alt-screen skip,
flood sampling + window recovery, tolerant rule decode, both
pipelines. Suite: 1904 green.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Per-host/per-command theme profiles: re-theme a pane over dangerous ssh (#176)

* Per-host/per-command theme profiles: re-theme a pane over dangerous ssh

A pane re-themes itself while it's somewhere dangerous — the classic
red-over-production safeguard. Configured as a 'profiles' array in
settings.json (first match wins; reload-on-save applies live):

  {"host": "*.prod.example.com", "theme": "Red Alert"}
  {"command": "ssh", "theme": "Dracula"}

Matching vantage:
- host: glob over the pane's OSC 7 hostname. The engine now surfaces
  the authority component (onRemoteHostChange, change-only emission,
  lowercased; nil for authority-less reports; RIS drops the state
  with a nil emission so respawns re-report cleanly). An ssh
  session's integration flips it to the remote; the local shell's
  OSC 7 flips it back on exit.
- command: glob over the pane's foreground process name (the
  existing SurfaceShellTracker vantage via tab.currentCommand —
  re-evaluated on every snapshot sync).

The override is the audit roadmap's sanctioned per-surface layer:
TerminalHostView.profileThemeOverride resolves the CANVAS through
the profile's theme — window chrome keeps the global theme, two
panes can wear different profiles, and nothing is ever written back
to settings. While overridden, the global custom canvas/palette/
selection color overrides and macOS-system light/dark resolution
are deliberately neutralized — a masked 'Red Alert' would defeat
the point.

ProfileRule matching is pure + glob via POSIX fnmatch (case-folded
manually for Linux parity); a rule with no criteria never matches
(it would silently re-theme everything); tolerant per-field decode
like TriggerRule.

Tests: glob/conjunction/guard matching, tolerant decode, settings
round-trip, OSC 7 host extraction (change-only, lowercased,
authority-less nil-once, RIS clear + re-emit, rejected payloads
silent). Suite: 1903 green.

* Normalize empty OSC 7 authority to nil host (Linux Foundation parity)

URL.host returns nil for an empty authority on Darwin but "" on
swift-corelibs-foundation, so file:///tmp reported Optional("") on
Linux and tripped testAuthoritylessReportEmitsNilOnce. Flat-map the
empty string to nil so both platforms honor the authority-less =
"locally known" contract identically.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Command palette covers the full command vocabulary (#173)

The Cmd+K palette listed ~20 curated actions; every other verb needed
the : prompt or a binding from memory. It now derives a Commands
section from CommandParser.knownVerbs — the same catalog that backs
bind-key, the : prompt, hooks, and list-commands — so a new bindable
verb lands in the palette automatically (no hand-maintained copy).

- Argument-less verbs run directly via the parsed Command through
  MainExecutor.executeSurfacingErrors (the : prompt's path).
- Argument-requiring verbs open the command prompt pre-filled with
  '<verb> ' (reusing presentTemplate). That includes four verbs that
  PARSE bare but to empty-arg no-ops (select-layout, send-keys,
  display-message, display-menu) — a probe of every verb's bare parse
  found those would silently do nothing as direct rows.
- Rows display live key bindings: present() -> buildActions() reloads
  keybindings.json each open and maps prefix/root bindings to the
  bound command's canonical text ('Prefix x' / bare spec).
- Themes: the full 490-theme catalog is reachable by SEARCH; the
  unfiltered browse view keeps only the featured set (a searchOnly
  flag on palette rows) so it doesn't bury everything else.
- Curated rows keep their richer titles/handlers; their verbs are
  excluded from the derived section (guard-tested so the exclusion
  set can't drift from the vocabulary).

CommandPaletteCatalog is pure (no coordinator access) and unit-
tested: completeness vs knownVerbs, no duplicates, direct-vs-prompt
classification, binding display formatting. Suite: 1897 green.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Hover-reveal pane close button on split panes (#168) (#171)

The second half of #168: there was no mouse affordance to close one
pane of a split — the user had to know prefix-x or close the whole
tab.

A small x now sits in each pane's top-right corner, visible ONLY
while the pointer hovers that corner region and ONLY when the tab
has more than one pane (single-pane tabs have the tab close button;
panes stay chrome-free at rest, per the no-pane-chrome rule — the
tab dot remains the only persistent indicator).

- PaneCloseOverlay (kit): 18pt xmark.circle.fill in a 56x40 hover
  region, faded in/out at 0.15s, canvas-foreground tinted like the
  other overlays. hitTest passes everything through except the
  button itself while revealed, and everything while hidden.
- TerminalHostView: corner NSTrackingArea armed only while
  showsPaneCloseAffordance is set (no tracking churn on single-pane
  tabs); onPaneCloseRequested surfaces the click.
- SessionCoordinator.killPane(surfaceID:) resolves the pane under
  the x (not the active pane) across all workspaces/tabs and issues
  the regular .killPane daemon command — identical semantics to
  prefix-x.
- ContentAreaViewController arms/disarms the affordance on every
  structural (re)mount, so closing down to one pane disarms the
  survivor.

Tests: overlay hidden + click-through at rest, reveal/hide
restores pass-through, only the button is clickable, click fires
the callback. Full suite green (1898).

author: Robert Courson
message:
Measure end-to-end typing latency: in-app echo percentiles + daemon RTT bench (#174)

The SCORECARD's input-to-photon section measured the present path
(p50 1.2ms) but never keystroke-to-photon end to end, and the daemon
round trip (the unmeasured half) had no probe at all. Measurement
only — no behavior changes; any vsync-pacing work stays a separate,
data-justified follow-up.

- FrameSignposter gains echo sampling: the three PTY-bound input
  sites (keyDown special keys, keyDown encoder path, insertText for
  plain typing) arm one pending sample; the FIRST present after it
  records keyDown -> present-completion. A present landing >500ms
  later is unrelated (dead key, swallowed shortcut, blink tick) and
  drops the sample instead of mis-attributing. Logged as
  'echo µs p50/p95/p99' every 60 keystrokes under the existing
  HARNESS_FRAME_SIGNPOSTS=1 gate — zero overhead otherwise.
- New EchoRTTBenchmark (HARNESS_BENCHMARKS + HARNESS_LIVE_DAEMON_TESTS
  gated): in-process DaemonServer + /bin/cat surface, measures client
  input frame -> PTY write -> kernel tty echo -> output frame over
  100 probes. First numbers: p50 437us, p95 1.9ms, p99 3.1ms (200us
  receive-poll inflates slightly). Combined with the present path,
  median typing latency ~1.6-2ms — under a 120Hz frame.
- SCORECARD: new 'Daemon echo RTT' section with methodology.

Three of the originally-planned items were already done or refuted
on inspection: StartupMetrics.firstSnapshot is already marked in
syncFromDaemon, Kitty image storage already has a byte cap with LRU
eviction (ImageLimits.maxBytesPerScreen), and AgentDetector's
nonisolated(unsafe) statics are all NSLock-protected.

Tests: echo sampler arm/consume/window/disabled semantics (injectable
clock); the RTT bench asserts a sanity ceiling. Suite: 1898 green.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Prompt navigation: Cmd+Up/Down jumps, select-last-output, #{command_duration} (#172)

OSC 133 shell integration has been engine-complete for a while (prompt
marks, exit-status gutter, copy-mode [ ] jumps) but had almost no
surface in the GUI. Three additions:

- View menu: Previous Prompt (Cmd+Up) / Next Prompt (Cmd+Down) drive
  the existing jump-previous/next-prompt commands — the Terminal.app/
  iTerm2 convention. App-level key equivalents, so the tmux-default-
  empty root key table stays empty (bind-key -T root still available).
- select-last-output (View > Select Last Command Output, Shift+Cmd+A):
  selects the lines strictly between the last two prompt marks and
  scrolls to reveal them when off-screen. Rides the content-anchored
  selection model (#161), so extraction works even when the output is
  outside the viewport. Bindable + : prompt + palette like any verb;
  client-local in the translator (same class as the jump verbs).
- #{command_duration}: the last finished command's runtime as a format
  token (850ms / 12s / 3m 5s), filled from the GUI's OSC 133 timing
  (host-delegate vantage; daemon/CLI contexts render empty, the
  documented partial-vantage convention). The command-finished
  notification already carried the duration — now formats can too.

Tests: select-last-output happy path, no-marks/single-mark/empty-
output no-ops, off-screen reveal + extraction; command_duration
formatting tiers + empty-unset; knownVerbs guard covers the new verb.
Suite: 1903 green. Stacked on #170 (absolute selection anchors).

author: Robert Courson
message:
Selection anchors on content, not the viewport: survive scrolling (#161) (#170)

Selecting text and then scrolling cleared the selection:
scrollByContinuous() (and the prompt jumps) called clearSelection()
unconditionally, because the anchors were viewport-row based and had
no meaning once the viewport moved.

Anchors are now ABSOLUTE buffer coordinates (line = virtual buffer
line, copy mode's space), converted from viewport rows at the mouse
event and rebased back at render:

- resolveAbsoluteSelectionRegion: ordering + granularity expansion +
  eviction clamping in buffer space, shared by render and extraction
- resolveSelectionRegion rebases to viewport rows on the emulator
  queue at build time (out-of-viewport rows just don't shade — the
  overlay pass already clamps per row, proven by the existing
  testOffViewportSelectionIsClamped)
- extraction reads bufferLine() in absolute space, so Cmd+C copies a
  selection that has scrolled out of the viewport (the old viewport-
  snapshot read came back blank)
- triple-click logical-line span is no longer clamped to the viewport
  (the off-screen wrapped tail belongs to the selection)
- the scroll paths keep the selection; typing/paste/resize still
  clear, unchanged
- updateResizePreview and the Cmd+C guard check selectionAnchor
  instead of resolving a region (the getter can emulatorSync — never
  on a resize-drag tick)
- main-thread conversions read historyCountMirror on the off-main
  pipeline (scrollByContinuous's discipline): no queue.sync from
  mouse handlers

Bench: selection_drag_overlay_pass / baked_rebuild medians within 1%
of main (5-run A/B). Tests: persistence across discrete + fractional
scrolling on both pipelines, content-anchored under new output,
eviction-clamp flood, and a Metal-gated presented-frame check that
the shading follows content across a scroll.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Replay must not re-fire side effects: fix stray query replies on reopen (#168) (#169)

On (re)attach, persisted scrollback was replayed through the local
emulator, which re-answered every query embedded in the history —
p10k's DECRQM 2026/2027 and kitty-keyboard probes came back through
onResponse -> InputGate -> PTY as junk typed at the prompt
(2026;2$y2027;0$y1u1u), long after anything was reading them.

TerminalEmulator gains an isReplaying flag, set by the host around
exactly the replayed chunk's feed on the emulator's serialized
context. While replaying:
- respond() emits nothing (one chokepoint for DECRQM/DA/DSR/kitty/
  XTGETTCAP/OSC color queries — no live reader exists)
- bells, OSC 9/777 notifications, OSC 52 clipboard writes, and
  OSC 133 command-finished reports stay quiet instead of re-firing
  on every reopen

State restoration is untouched: grid, modes, title, cwd, user vars,
prompt marks, and progress (stale-timeout guarded) all still apply.
A query split across the replay->live boundary — a program actively
waiting while we attach — still answers, because parser state
carries over and respond() fires during the live chunk.

Both feed pipelines (sync and off-main) bracket the flag; the CLI
attach-window compositor never wires onResponse, so it was already
safe. Engine + kit regression tests cover suppression, restoration,
and the boundary-spanning case.

author: robzilla1738
message:
Release v1.11.0 (build 125)

Engine efficiency pass (#162-#167): OSC byte routing + bulk consume,
streamed scrollback compaction, find-bar allocation diet, coalesced
main hops, renderer encode cleanups, hyperlink registry alias fix.
Benchmark baselines re-recorded post-merge (5 new benches; the two
>30% movers — glyph_atlas_mixed_unicode, render_encode_incremental —
verified environmental: pre-pass code reproduces today's numbers).

author: Robert Courson
message:
Renderer micro-batch: drop per-frame allocations on the encode path (#167)

Five small, independently-revertable cleanups in TerminalMetalRenderer:

- emitLigatedGlyphs: the run accumulation String + utf16ToColumn array
  allocated per run per dirty row per frame; now instance-scratch buffers
  reused via removeAll(keepingCapacity:) (encode is single-threaded per
  renderer).
- instanceUploadCacheKey: the prompt-gutter .map + .sorted ran on every
  encode even when the gutter was empty/unchanged; now fast-paths empty
  and memoizes the sorted array keyed on the gutter dictionary. The key
  stays value-identical (the stable-frame cache equality depends on it).
- Salvage dirty-row IndexSet: built by inserting directly instead of
  materializing the intermediate filter array.
- Splice dirty spans: appended non-empty at the source, dropping the
  three per-splice-frame .filter { !$0.isEmpty } intermediates.
- Row content keys: a salvage pass already hashes every candidate row
  against the current frame (O(columns) each); the encode pass in the
  same build now reuses those keys when stamping salvage-missed rows
  instead of re-walking the columns (per-build memo, reset every build).

Instance output is byte-identical (render stats fields unchanged across
the bench suite); kit + renderer + compositor-parity suites green (315
tests). Individual deltas are below render-bench noise by design — the
win is allocation pressure on the per-frame path.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Off-main output path: coalesce the per-chunk main hop (#166)

receiveOffMain dispatched an unconditional DispatchQueue.main.async per
output chunk, whose only job was refreshing the input-side mirrors and
poking the render scheduler. Under a flood that is tens of thousands of
main dispatches per second of pure scheduling tax, contending with input,
render, and UI work on main.

Now each chunk merges its post-parse state into a lock-guarded staged hop
(PendingMainHop in SurfaceEmulatorState, same tokenLock discipline as the
frame tokens); only the chunk that finds no hop in flight dispatches one,
and the hop applies the LATEST staged state. addedHistory accumulates
across coalesced chunks (the scrolled-up anchor must advance by the total
lines pushed, and only positive deltas count, matching the old per-chunk
guard); modes/alt-screen/historyCount are latest-wins, which is exactly
what the old FIFO per-chunk delivery converged on. DEC 2026 applies the
final state of the window (a mid-burst open/close collapses; the end
state is identical). A lone keystroke echo on an idle surface still gets
its immediate hop + presentNow. testingMainHopCount now counts executed
hops; testingWaitForEmulatorIdle flushes the staged hop so tests never
observe half-converged mirrors. The main-thread receive() sibling is
untouched.

consume_latency_under_load (release): mainHops 1025 -> 1 (4 KiB chunks)
and 65 -> 1 (64 KiB), mainGapMax ~9.5us -> ~5us; throughput and
echo_latency_local flat (the harness main thread is idle; the win is
scheduling tax + contention in the real app). New worker tests: burst
coalescing + mirror convergence, scrolled-up anchor advances by total
added history, 2026 flip-within-burst applies final state. Full suite:
1883 tests green.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Find-bar search: per-cell unit resolver replaces per-cell String builds (#165)

Every find-bar keystroke re-runs the search over the whole buffer on main
inside emulatorSync, and the old hay construction built a fresh String per
cell per line (cell.cluster is a computed property allocating each access,
folded through another allocation), plus a fresh [String] hay array per
line.

A shared UnitResolver now derives each cell's search unit:
- printable ASCII (the overwhelming majority) indexes a static 128-entry
  table (exact + folded variants),
- other no-mark cells memoize by codepoint (box-drawing/CJK fills repeat
  heavily),
- only mark-carrying cells pay the cluster build + NFC, exactly as before.
The hay buffers (array for substring, String + cellStarts for regex) are
reused across lines. Needle segmentation (incl. the Thai SARA AM split) is
untouched.

buffer_search_keystroke bench over 20k mixed lines (release, 3-run
medians): substring 50.5ms -> 27.2ms (1.9x), regex 105.4ms -> 87.5ms.
TerminalBufferSearchTests + ThaiCombiningMarkTests element-identical;
engine suite 487 green.

author: Robert Courson
message:
OSC dispatch goes bytewise: no String round-trip or per-byte consume for bulk payloads (#164)

An OSC 1337 inline image (or OSC 52 clipboard set) carries a multi-megabyte
base64 body. The old path cost three full passes before the base64 decode
even started: parserOSC decoded the WHOLE payload to a String (UTF-8
validation + copy), handleITerm2Image re-encoded it to Array(payload.utf8),
and ITerm2InlineImage.parse decoded it to a String again. On top of that,
the parser consumed every OSC payload byte through the per-byte state
machine, with the oscCap computed property evaluated per byte.

- parserOSC now routes by code bytewise; 1337 image and 52 clipboard
  payloads stay byte slices end to end (base64 decoded via
  Data(base64Encoded: Data(slice))). Small-payload codes (title, 7, 8, 9,
  133, 777, 4, 10-12, 22) materialize a String exactly as before,
  including the drop-on-invalid-UTF-8 behavior.
- feedBuffer grows a bulk OSC-run branch: inside an OSC string only
  BEL/ESC/CAN/SUB can affect parsing, so the payload is scanned to the
  next delimiter (SIMD16) and appended in one go. Byte-identical to the
  per-byte path: the first 5 bytes go singly so the 1337-prefix cap sniff
  is settled, and the bulk append is clamped to the same cap.
- ITerm2InlineImage.parse gains an ArraySlice overload that only ever
  builds a String for the short key=value args segment.

Behavior pins: a non-UTF-8 byte inside a 1337 base64 body no longer drops
the whole image (it is base64 junk, skipped via ignoreUnknownCharacters);
String-routed codes still drop invalid UTF-8 wholesale — both covered by
new tests.

New benches (release): osc_1337_inline_image_feed 4 MiB in 5.2ms,
osc_52_clipboard_feed 800 KiB in 0.43ms. Engine suite: 489 tests green.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Scrollback compaction streams the tail instead of reading the whole log (#163)

compact() and the init-path compaction did Data(contentsOf:) of the ENTIRE
log on the serial queue appends ride, just to keep suffix(retentionCap).
With unlimited scrollback the cap is 512 MiB and high-water 1 GiB — a
single compaction could pull a gigabyte into the daemon while persistence
stalled behind it.

Both paths now share compactToTail: seek to (size - retentionCap) and
stream the tail to the temp file in 4 MiB chunks, so reads are
O(retentionCap) and peak resident memory is one chunk. The durability
dance is unchanged (temp alongside the log, owner-only before any content
lands, fsync, POSIX rename(2)); loadTail seek-reads only the requested
tail as well.

New tests: byte-exact suffix across odd-sized chunk boundaries, owner-only
perms on the compacted log. New bench scrollback_compaction_64mib
(HarnessBenchmarks now links HarnessDaemonCore): 8.8ms for 64 MiB -> 16 MiB.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Hyperlink registry: never alias stale cell IDs after the flood purge (#162)

The 16,384-entry flood bound cleared the registry AND reset nextHyperlinkID
to 1, so cells still carrying pre-purge IDs would silently resolve to
whatever new URL landed on the recycled ID. Keep the purge but let the ID
counter run monotonically: stale IDs now resolve nil (dead link).

Also replace the synthesized "id=...\u{1}uri" String key with a small
Hashable HyperlinkKey struct, dropping the per-OSC-8 interpolation
allocation and the double-keyed string compare.

author: robzilla1738
message:
Release v1.10.0 (build 124)

CHANGELOG rolled, Info.plist + HarnessVersion.swift bumped together, what's-new
banner regenerated (Added: 9, Changed: 3, Fixed: 13, Performance: 8, Removed: 1).

author: Robert Courson
message:
Appearance mode: macOS flip re-skins the canvas; cleared overrides stay cleared (#160)

* Appearance mode: macOS flip re-skins the terminal canvas; cleared color overrides stay cleared

Two defects found by live testing the System appearance mode end-to-end (per-PR
review and the static seam audit could not see either):

1. Flipping macOS light/dark never re-skinned the TERMINAL CANVAS — the appearance
   handler refreshed window chrome and border colors only, so the canvas kept the
   pre-flip palette until an unrelated settings change forced a reapply. The handler
   now routes through the same full host re-apply the settings path uses
   (updateChromeAndHosts, parameterized with the explicit system appearance), hops
   one runloop turn so all readers see the settled appearance, and a distributed
   AppleInterfaceThemeChangedNotification observer backstops the KVO path.

2. Cleared color overrides resurrected from the source-terminal import on every
   launch: JSONEncoder omits nil keys, and the color fields decoded through the
   import-aware fallback — 'user cleared this' and 'never set' were
   indistinguishable, so clearThemeColorOverrides() (which the appearance-mode
   switch relies on) silently un-cleared at next boot on any machine with a Ghostty
   config. Color overrides now decode plainly (absent = nil); first-run/backfill
   import remains exclusively load()'s explicit signature-gated path. Regression
   test pins the round-trip.

Live-verified: boot in light resolves the light system theme, OS flips re-skin
canvas + chrome both directions with no relaunch and no stale colors.

* CHANGELOG: appearance flip re-skin + durable cleared overrides

* Tests: update the refreshChromePalette source pin for the parameterized call

author: Robert Courson
message:
Receipts: bench gate methodology + median baselines + first real scorecard (#159)

* bench: warm-encode gate for render_encode_stats, documented methodology, median re-record

render_encode_stats_160x48 timed a single COLD-ATLAS shot: bimodal, machine-state
sensitive (field data: 1.19ms baseline read 2.8ms on a post-idle run), and it turned
any rasterizer change into an encode 'regression'. It now encodes a content-shifted
warmup frame first — the atlas warms but all 48 rows still re-encode (different
content keys), so the gated number is the warm full encode. Cold rasterization cost
stays covered by the glyph_atlas_* benches. New baseline 809us (was 1,186us cold).

Scripts/benchmarks/README.md now documents the gate protocol learned the hard way:
judge the second of two back-to-back runs (first heats the machine), a real
regression must REPRODUCE across consecutive runs (flapping sets are variance —
observed: 13 'regressions' on a cold post-idle run, a nearly disjoint 6 on the next,
one run's 1.74x regression reading 0.71x improved the run after), record = median of
three warm runs, CI's bench job stays advisory.

Baselines re-recorded accordingly (median-of-three warm runs, 60 benches; spread
analysis across five runs showed every flag inside its own per-bench variance, e.g.
render_encode_incremental_damage recorded [118.8/179.3/139.0]us in one session).

scorecard.sh cold-start now kills straggler preview daemons and drops the socket/pid
between launches — debug builds spawn the daemon as a child process, and an orphan
from a previous iteration would keep daemonConnected/firstSnapshot artificially warm.

* scorecard: first real Harness-vs-Ghostty receipts (M1 Pro, v1.10 tip)

Cold start: Harness firstWindow 127ms / firstDrawable 117ms (debug preview build)
vs Ghostty 288ms wall-to-window. Throughput: issue #27's ansi_sgr/attributes/unicode
losses are GONE (within single digits of release Ghostty, from a debug build) —
the #31 parse speedups + #139 UCD width tables closed it; redraw +80% and truecolor
+121% in Harness's favor (frame coalescing), scrollback ahead. Input-to-photon:
present p50 1.2ms, zero dropped frames through live resize drag + scroll fling.
Memory recorded with the retention asymmetry stated (Harness keeps all 1M lines by
design; Ghostty's default cap keeps the tail). Idle-power pending sudo on the
measurement box; all probe asymmetries stated inline.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Pre-release polish: lenient enum decode everywhere, blink-cache reset, zero warnings (#158)

- Every String-raw enum settings key now decodes leniently (unknown raw value falls
  back per-field) instead of failing the whole file into the corrupt-backup path;
  bespoke fallbacks survive (.full experience-upgrade semantic, .automatic notch).
  A sweep test feeds garbage through all ten enum keys at once.
- invalidateRenderGeneration() resets the blink-scan cache so the occlusion path
  cannot re-arm the text-blink timer from a stale frame after a theme/font change
  or window detach — every invalidating site schedules the render that recomputes
  the truth.
- FileWatcher is @unchecked Sendable (queue-confined, invariant documented) and
  ThemeImportController discards its non-fatal install result explicitly: the
  release build is zero-warning again.
- Regression guard: modified keypad keys under kitty report functional codepoints
  with modifiers (CSI 57404;2u / 57413;5u), pinning the spec-correct path.

author: Robert Courson
message:
Option key types composed characters by default; add macos-option-as-alt setting (#156)

option+L / option+7 now type the layout's @ / | (dead keys included) instead of
unconditionally Esc-prefixing — matching Terminal.app/iTerm2/Ghostty/kitty. A new
optionAsMeta setting (composed / meta / left / right, Settings ▸ Keys ▸ Option Key)
restores Meta for readline users, and Ghostty's macos-option-as-alt now imports,
left/right split forms included (importer signature v7).

The strip happens at the view seam — the InputEncoder contract is unchanged: special
keys (opt+arrow/backspace word motion) keep their Esc encodings in every mode,
Ctrl+Option combos never compose, and kitty-protocol reports drop the alt bit exactly
when Option participated in composition (kitty semantics). String-raw enum settings
decode leniently via a new FieldDecoder.decodeEnum so an unknown raw value falls back
to the field default instead of failing the whole settings decode.

Fixes #155.

author: Robert Courson
message:
docs: v1.10 truth pass (CHANGELOG externals, roadmap close-out, ledger sync) (#157)

* docs: v1.10 truth pass — CHANGELOG externals, roadmap close-out, handbook/README/parity sync

The Unreleased changelog covered the #140–#154 queue but not the three external
PRs that merged the same day: #46 (prefix ( / ) session navigation), #48
(TerminalFontResolver + crisp-mode glyph thickening), #58 (system appearance
mode). Add those entries, close out docs/V1_10_ROADMAP.md (header status +
per-PR shipped markers, AUDIT_ROADMAP style), sync the handbook (font resolver,
thicken in the textRendering rows, session-nav prefix keys, the scorecard as
the comparative-receipts harness), update README's auto light/dark bullet to
the #58 reality, and record the switch-client -n/-p adaptation in the tmux
parity ledger.

* tests+comments: name the scrollback absence window, justify the IPCReadBuffer unwrap

ScrollbackPersistenceTests repeated a magic usleep(700_000) at four
bounded-absence sites; hoist it into one documented file-scope constant
(absenceWindowMicros) — no behavior change, suite green under
HARNESS_LIVE_DAEMON_TESTS=1. IPCReadBuffer.payloadData gets a one-line
safety note for the force-unwrapped baseAddress (count > 0 guard + the
caller's bounds invariant make storage non-empty).

* Preserve explicit settings during import migration

Ultraworked with [Sisyphus](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>

* docs: trim the off-topic appearance prose from README and the multiplexer guide

The added README "Appearance defaults" section duplicated the existing
auto light/dark feature bullet, and an appearance-settings section does
not belong in the multiplexer guide. The durable documentation for the
new behavior lives where it fits: MIGRATION.md (split-theme import +
fingerprint-change semantics) and the AGENT-HANDBOOK settings table
(appearanceMode/systemLightThemeName/systemDarkThemeName). Restores the
guide's section numbering.

* settings: behavior-level coverage for the macOS-appearance toggle seeding rule

seedUnsetSystemThemeNames becomes a static seam so the tests exercise the real
rule without instantiating the view controller: only blank names are seeded, the
active theme becomes the dark half only when it actually exists in the catalog.

---------

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>
Co-authored-by: robzilla1738 <robertcourson1738@gmail.com>

Ultraworked with [Sisyphus](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>

* Keep crisp text thickening synthetic

Ultraworked with [Sisyphus](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>

* Wire crisp text to surface thickening

Ultraworked with [Sisyphus](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent)

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>

* Apply crisp-mode thickening to composed clusters too

Multi-scalar grapheme clusters (Thai base + vowel + tone) rasterize through
the CTLineDraw composition path, which skipped the synthetic thickening the
single-glyph and shaped-ligature paths apply — so crisp-mode composed text
rendered visibly thinner than its neighbors. Route the cluster path through
the same thickenCoverage pass. Thicken off (the default) stays byte-identical.

---------

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>
Co-authored-by: robzilla1738 <robertcourson1738@gmail.com>

author: robzilla1738
message:
bench: median-of-three baselines (last-wins per run, matching the compare script)

Single-sample baselines flaked the 15% gate on identical code — micro-benchmarks
(sub-10us stalls, ~50us previews) swing up to 2.9x run-to-run on a warm machine.
Three full runs, per-benchmark median with the script's own last-wins-per-run
parse rule (reflow_cost_width prints parameterized sizes; mixing them would skew).

author: robzilla1738
message:
bench: re-record baselines on owner hardware after the v1.10 queue

Adds the two new gates the queue introduced (build_frame_search_highlights_160x48,
build_frame_combining_heavy_160x48) and refreshes the other 58: the committed numbers
had drifted from what current main measures on this machine (pre-queue main already
read ~1.25x the old build_frame baseline), so per-PR regressions were A/B-measured
against main directly during review — the one real regression that surfaced
(PR-27's per-cell interval cursor taxing the plain build path ~20%) was fixed at the
source before merge rather than absorbed into these numbers.

author: robzilla1738
message:
docs: consolidate duplicate Unreleased headings after the v1.10 queue merges

Union-merging fourteen branches' CHANGELOG entries left Added/Changed appearing
twice under Unreleased; one section each, entries preserved verbatim.
